title: Auto-compute orders.filled from billed leads (port Railway FIFO)
brief: The casesdaily admin Orders tab currently stores `filled` as a manually-entered number. The original Railway dashboard auto-computes `filled` from billed leads (FIFO per client). Port that logic. Filled is derived, never written. Status flips to 'Filled' automatically when filled >= ordered. Show '— auto-computed from billed leads' note. The data path: enriched ghl_leads where assignedClient === order.client AND clientBilled === true. Sort billed leads oldest first; walk orders sorted by date_opened ascending; consume slots until ordered is reached, then move to next order; overflow leads tagged unattributed. The OrdersTable.tsx is client-side and fetches /api/admin/dashboard/orders. We need (a) a new server-side endpoint that returns orders with computed filled, OR (b) compute on the client by also fetching enriched leads. Recommend (a) — clean, single trip. Output a complete drop-in replacement for /api/admin/dashboard/orders/route.ts and the OrdersTable.tsx component. Remove the 'Filled' input from the modal — it must not be editable. Keep ordered, price_per_lead, payment_received, payment_type, status, date_opened, notes editable. status: auto-flip to 'Filled' is acceptable but allow user override. Add a small expanded row callout listing the billed lead ids attributed to that order (top 5).
outputContract:
Produce a single Markdown response with these sections:

1. **Plan** (5–10 lines) — name files changed/created, summarize the data flow.
2. **Files** — one fenced code block per file using the form:
   ```typescript
   // FILE: <repo-relative-path>
   <full file contents>
   ```
   Required files: the new GET route at `casesdaily/src/app/api/admin/dashboard/orders/route.ts` (returning enriched orders with computed `filled`, `outstanding`, `attributedLeadIds[]`), and the updated `casesdaily/src/components/admin/dashboard/crud/OrdersTable.tsx` (no manual filled input).
3. **Migration note** — if any new types need to be added to the OrderRow interface, include them inline.

Use `EnrichedGHLLead` (flat camelCase) when reading lead data. Use the existing Supabase service client (see existing route patterns). Match the existing code style (no shadcn, plain Tailwind, server actions if useful, but keep client component for OrdersTable).
